title: 20th anniversary of European Sustainable Energy Week welcomes all EU energy commissioners since 2007
content: The theme of this year’s event is 'A Clean, Secure and Competitive Energy Union'.
snippet: The theme of this year’s event is 'A Clean, Secure and Competitive Energy Union'.

title: Gas Market Task Force presents its findings on the functioning of EU gas and gas derivatives markets
content: A dedicated group of EU gas experts drawn together to scrutinise the way that EU gas and gas derivatives markets work in practice has presented its analysis in a special report published by the Commission.
snippet: A dedicated group of EU gas experts drawn together to scrutinise the way that EU gas and gas derivatives markets work in practice has presented its analysis in a special report published by the

title: Commission welcomes political agreement on the Return Regulation
content: The Commission welcomes the political agreement reached between the European Parliament and the Council on 1 June 2026 on the Regulation establishing a new Common European System for Returns, providing Member States with the necessary tools to make returns more efficient.
snippet: The Commission welcomes the political agreement reached between the European Parliament and the Council on 1 June 2026 on the Regulation establishing a new Common European System for Returns,

title: Commission approves €54 million Spanish State aid for agricultural companies facing increased fuel prices
content: The European Commission has approved a €54 million Spanish State aid scheme to support agricultural companies facing increased fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis.
snippet: The European Commission has approved a €54 million Spanish State aid scheme to support agricultural companies facing increased fuel prices due to the Middle East crisis.

title: EU agri-food trade surplus expands in February
content: The latest monthly agri-food trade report shows developments in imports and exports of EU agri-food products in February 2026.
snippet: The latest monthly agri-food trade report shows developments in imports and exports of EU agri-food products in February 2026.

title: Continuous progress across European Research Area priorities, but structural challenges remain
content: The new ERA Scoreboard evaluates performance across the four ERA priorities, offering robust evidence to support policymaking at both EU and national levels.
snippet: The new ERA Scoreboard evaluates performance across the four ERA priorities, offering robust evidence to support policymaking at both EU and national levels.

title: Innovative techniques: What works best for making energy-intensive industries more sustainable?
content: A new JRC analysis identifies and maps market-ready or nearly ready innovative techniques capable of reducing the environmental impact of energy-intensive industries in the EU.
snippet: A new JRC analysis identifies and maps market-ready or nearly ready innovative techniques capable of reducing the environmental impact of energy-intensive industries in the EU.

title: Commission accelerates transition away from animal testing in chemical safety assessments
content: A new roadmap phasing out the use of animals in chemical safety assessments has been presented.
snippet: A new roadmap phasing out the use of animals in chemical safety assessments has been presented.

title: Eurobarometer highlights obstacles and opportunities for European SMEs recruiting outside the EU
content: A new Eurobarometer survey reveals that 46% of European small and medium-sized enterprises face challenges in finding workers with the right skills. Recruitment from outside the EU remains limited, only 14% of SMEs attempting to hire non-EU nationals.
snippet: A new Eurobarometer survey reveals that 46% of European small and medium-sized enterprises face challenges in finding workers with the right skills. Recruitment from outside the EU remains limited,

title: The EU advance cargo information system (ICS2) helps customs catch smugglers, fraudsters, and criminal activity
content: ICS2 is already providing concrete benefits for customs authorities on the frontline, protecting citizens and the EU economy.  Take a look at examples of ICS2 in action in 2025.
snippet: ICS2 is already providing concrete benefits for customs authorities on the frontline, protecting citizens and the EU economy. Take a look at examples of ICS2 in action in 2025.

title: DG Translation in a city near you: June events
content: Join one of our events in June!
snippet: Join one of our events in June!

title: EU Granted Full Ownership of Galileo Antenna located at the European Space Security and Education Centre in Redu
content: Today, the European Union, represented by the European Commission, formalised the transfer of full ownership of the Telemetry, Tracking and Control (TT&C)-5 Antenna, also known as RED-1 TTC, from the European Space Agency (ESA) to the European Union.
snippet: Today, the European Union, represented by the European Commission, formalised the transfer of full ownership of the Telemetry, Tracking and Control (TT&C)-5 Antenna, also known as RED-1 TTC, from the

title: 190 million EU ETS allowances to be placed into the Market Stability Reserve between September 2026 and August 2027
content: The European Commission has adopted a Communication on the total number of allowances in circulation (TNAC) on the European carbon market.
snippet: The European Commission has adopted a Communication on the total number of allowances in circulation (TNAC) on the European carbon market.

title: 2025 Innovation Fund Net-Zero Technologies Call attracted strong interest from European industry with over 350 applications
content: The Innovation Fund 2025 Net-Zero Technologies (IF25 NZT) Call closed on 23 April 2026, receiving a total of 358 project proposals from 27 countries, confirming strong interest from European industry to innovate and decarbonise.
snippet: The Innovation Fund 2025 Net-Zero Technologies (IF25 NZT) Call closed on 23 April 2026, receiving a total of 358 project proposals from 27 countries, confirming strong interest from European industry

title: Improving energy efficiency of buildings to reduce bills and save energy
content: New rules to improve the energy performance of buildings across the EU come into effect on 30 May 2026.
snippet: New rules to improve the energy performance of buildings across the EU come into effect on 30 May 2026.

title: Commission approves €9 billion Spanish capacity mechanism for security of electricity supply
content: The European Commission has approved, under EU State aid rules, a €9 billion capacity mechanism for electricity supply for Spain.
snippet: The European Commission has approved, under EU State aid rules, a €9 billion capacity mechanism for electricity supply for Spain.

title: High-level roundtable explores intertwined challenges of climate resilience, defence and security
content: On 26 May, the European Commission and the European External Action Service hosted a high-level meeting to discuss the links between climate resilience, security and defence.
snippet: On 26 May, the European Commission and the European External Action Service hosted a high-level meeting to discuss the links between climate resilience, security and defence.

title: Commission fines Temu
content: The European Commission issued a fine of €200 million to Temu under the Digital Services Act (DSA). The evidence at the disposal indicates that consumers in the EU are very likely to encounter illegal items on Temu. Temu has until 28 August 2026 to submit an action plan.
snippet: The European Commission issued a fine of €200 million to Temu under the Digital Services Act (DSA). The evidence at the disposal indicates that consumers in the EU are very likely to encounter illegal

title: Schengen short-stay Visa applications rise in 2025 but remain below pre-pandemic levels
content: According to figures released by the Commission, EU and Schengen-associated countries received nearly 12 million applications for short-stay visas in 2025, marking a 1.8% increase from 2024. However, demand remains well below the 17 million applications recorded in 2019 before the COVID-19 pandemic.

title: Commission opens consultation on territorial supply constraints
content: The European Commission has launched a public consultation to gather stakeholders and citizens' views on planned action to address territorial supply constraints.
snippet: The European Commission has launched a public consultation to gather stakeholders and citizens' views on planned action to address territorial supply constraints.

title: Commission opens in-depth foreign subsidies investigation into JD.com's proposed acquisition of CECONOMY
content: The European Commission has opened an in-depth investigation to assess, under the Foreign Subsidies Regulation ('FSR'), the proposed acquisition by JD.com, Inc. ('JD.com') of CECONOMY AG ('CECONOMY').
snippet: The European Commission has opened an in-depth investigation to assess, under the Foreign Subsidies Regulation ('FSR'), the proposed acquisition by JD.com, Inc. ('JD.com') of CECONOMY AG ('CECONOMY').

title: Package travel - Stronger rights for travellers and simpler rules for the travel industry
content: The new rules adopted by the European Parliament and the Council strengthen traveller rights and improve legal certainty for the package travel sector, which is largely made up of SMEs and micro-enterprises.
snippet: The new rules adopted by the European Parliament and the Council strengthen traveller rights and improve legal certainty for the package travel sector, which is largely made up of SMEs and

title: Sovereign Cloud Framework explained
content: Due to the large interest of public administrations and IT companies, the European Commission is providing a more detailed overview of its Cloud Sovereignty Framework, a tool it created to evaluate providers of sovereign cloud in recent tender.
snippet: Due to the large interest of public administrations and IT companies, the European Commission is providing a more detailed overview of its Cloud Sovereignty Framework, a tool it created to evaluate

title: Blue Economy key to Europe’s future prosperity says Shipping Minister
content: The blue economy will be central to Europe’s prosperity and resilience in the years ahead, according to Deputy Shipping Minister Marina Hadjimanolis.
snippet: The blue economy will be central to Europe’s prosperity and resilience in the years ahead, according to Deputy Shipping Minister Marina Hadjimanolis.

title: New EU rules harmonising transport emissions calculations take effect
content: The CountEmissionsEU framework entered into force, establishing the first EU-wide standardised methodology for calculating emissions from freight and passenger transport.
snippet: The CountEmissionsEU framework entered into force, establishing the first EU-wide standardised methodology for calculating emissions from freight and passenger transport.

title: New EU framework boosts Single Market resilience in times of crisis
content: The Internal Market Emergency and Resilience Act (IMERA) has entered into application, giving the European Union a structured framework to anticipate, prepare for and respond to crises that could disrupt the free movement of goods, services and people across the single market.
snippet: The Internal Market Emergency and Resilience Act (IMERA) has entered into application, giving the European Union a structured framework to anticipate, prepare for and respond to crises that could

title: New scoreboard shows success of European startup policies
content: The scoreboard reveals a clear trend: pro-startup policies drive real results. Since 2020, the baseline year for the Scoreboard, 20 out of 27 EU Member States have improved their performance, proving that targeted support for founders fuels innovation, job creation, and economic growth.
snippet: The scoreboard reveals a clear trend: pro-startup policies drive real results. Since 2020, the baseline year for the Scoreboard, 20 out of 27 EU Member States have improved their performance, proving

title: Council adopts first ever EU framework for science diplomacy
content: Today, the Council adopted a recommendation on a European Union framework for science diplomacy, following a proposal by the European Commission.
snippet: Today, the Council adopted a recommendation on a European Union framework for science diplomacy, following a proposal by the European Commission.

title: Eurostat: Energy use in the industry sector continues to decline
content: In 2024, the EU’s industry sector used 8 835 petajoules (PJ) of energy, an 8.1% decrease compared with 2014. The final energy consumption has been steadily declining since 1990.
snippet: In 2024, the EU’s industry sector used 8 835 petajoules (PJ) of energy, an 8.1% decrease compared with 2014. The final energy consumption has been steadily declining since 1990.

title: Europe for Aviation for more modern and efficient ATM in Europe
content: Nine European institutions showcased their joint vision and progress toward a more modern and effective air traffic management system in Europe at Airspace World, the annual three-day ATM trade fair that took place in Lisbon.
snippet: Nine European institutions showcased their joint vision and progress toward a more modern and effective air traffic management system in Europe at Airspace World, the annual three-day ATM trade fair

title: Poland receives first €6.6 billion payment under SAFE
content: Poland received its first payment of €6.6 billion under the Security Action for Europe (SAFE) defence financing instrument, representing 15% of its total allocation of €43.7 billion. Poland is the largest recipient of SAFE funding and the first Member State to receive a payment under the instrument.

title: Commission welcomes ENTSO-E report confirming EU electricity preparedness for summer
content: In its latest report Summer Outlook 2026, published today, the European Network for Transmission System Operators for Electricity (ENTSO-E) confirms that it does not foresee general risks to the security of electricity supply across most of the EU for the coming summer.
